message.content:
Differentiator titles and descriptions:
1. Dreamlike Visual-Sensory Shift: HIGH participants report brighter colors, foggy dreamlike vision, and heightened touch; LOW participants emphasize realistic perception with no color changes or dissociation.
HIGH transcripts consistently describe perceptual alteration: “colors are brighter,” “foggy vision,” and a feeling “like dreaming,” alongside somatic signs such as pleasant skin sensitivity, jaw clenching, pupil dilation, and perceived heart-rate increases. Even when acknowledging reality, HIGH participants say it “feels like a very pleasant dream.” In contrast, LOW participants repeatedly anchor their experience in realism and clarity: “I am in this world,” “things are quite real,” “I don’t feel colors change,” and they attribute blurred vision to other drugs (e.g., acid) rather than Molly/MDMA. LOW participants also frame intoxication as loss of mental control—which they do not experience—highlighting a clear, grounded, and non-dissociative phenomenology.

2. Setting-Driven Vigilance and Focus Drift: HIGH participants fixate on study context, mistrust, and fatigue, leading to concentration lapses; LOW participants feel secure, clear-headed, and sustain task engagement.
HIGH transcripts show pronounced meta-awareness of the research setting and professional boundaries (“not buddy-buddy”), explicit mistrust shaping their mood, and concerns about protocol confounds (sleeping in MRI, placebo effects, caffeine restrictions). They report “harder time concentrating,” getting “pulled out” of task patterns, “falling asleep,” and making more mistakes, with suggestions to modify protocols (e.g., caffeine). LOW participants, by contrast, emphasize security and reduced anxiety in the setting, describe being chatty and less defensive, and repeatedly claim mental clarity and intact task performance (“mental focus, no”), only noting occasional mind-wandering. Their attention is directed toward emotional openness rather than vigilance about study procedures, yielding steadier engagement.

Differentiator variable names:
sensory_dreamlike_shift,context_vigilance_focus_drift
